text: Spermatogenesis is a multiphase process of cellular transformation that produces mature male gametes known as sperm for sexual reproduction (Kang et al., 2015). The process of spermatogenesis can be broken down into 3 phases: the mitotic proliferation of spermatogonia, meiosis, and post meiotic differentiation(spermiogenesis) (Boulanger et al., 2015). Male fertility is dependent on the quantity as well as the proper cellular morphology of the sperm formed in the testes (Chen et al., 2020). The fusion of sperm and oocytes is the key step for the beginning of life known as fertilization (Alavi et al., 2019). Impaired spermatogenesis may impact fertility and, consequently, also reduce reproduction.
